title: Holcim, IGNIS P2X and Exolum Present ECO2FLY: A Pioneering Project for the Decarbonization of the Construction Materials and Air Transport Industries
content: ECO2FLY is a groundbreaking CO2 circular economy project developed by Holcim, IGNIS P2X, and Exolum, aimed at decarbonizing the construction and aviation industries. The project, based at Holcim's facility in Villaluenga de la Sagra (Toledo), will capture over 700,000 tons of CO2 annually during cement production. This CO2 will be combined with green hydrogen to produce 100,000 tons of sustainable aviation fuel (eSAF) per year, with the remaining CO2 stored in geological repositories. Over the first 10 years, ECO2FLY is expected to avoid over 6.5 million tons of CO2 emissions. The project positions ECO2FLY as a European leader in decarbonization and aims to create new industries, jobs, and wealth, contributing to Spain's energy independence and strengthening the EU's sustainability efforts.
author: Analysts Account
